steps.title: Connect genes to tools
steps.content: The passage first notes similar tools across Neolithic settlements, then adds Demirci et al.'s finding of genetic affinity among those populations. Genetic affinity supports a population connection, not just trade or imitation. That makes the genetic result an alternative to purely cultural explanations for the shared blade designs.

steps.title: Reject unsupported extensions
steps.content: A focuses on imitation, B invents a lack of other cultural similarities, and D adds migration direction and timing. The finding instead suggests tool similarities may relate to population blending. The answer is C.
